2604.19639.title: Safety-Critical Contextual Control via Online Riemannian Optimization with World Models
2604.19639.paper_summary: Modern world models are becoming too complex to admit explicit dynamical descriptions. We study safety-critical contextual control, where a Planner must optimize a task objective using only feasibility samples from a black-box Simulator, conditioned on a context signal $ξ_t$. We develop a sample-based Penalized Predictive Control (PPC) framework grounded in online Riemannian optimization, in which the Simulator compresses the feasibility manifold into a score-based density $\hat{p}(u \mid ξ_t)$ that endows the action space with a Riemannian geometry guiding the Planner's gradient descent. The barrier curvature $κ(ξ_t)$, the minimum curvature of the conditional log-density $-\ln\hat{p}(\cdot\midξ_t)$, governs both convergence rate and safety margin, replacing the Lipschitz constant of the unknown dynamics. Our main result is a contextual safety bound showing that the distance from the true feasibility manifold is controlled by the score estimation error and a ratio that depends on $κ(ξ_t)$, both of which improve with richer context. Simulations on a dynamic navigation task confirm that contextual PPC substantially outperforms marginal and frozen density models, with the advantage growing after environment shifts.
2604.19639.paper_summary_zh: 現代世界模型變得過於複雜，無法接受明確的動態描述。<lb/>我們研究安全關鍵的上下文控制，其中規劃者必須僅使用來自黑箱模擬器的可行性樣本來優化任務目標，這些樣本以上下文信號 $ξ_t$ 為條件。<lb/>我們開發了一種基於樣本的懲罰預測控制（PPC）框架，該框架基於在線黎曼優化，其中模擬器將可行性流形壓縮為基於分數的密度 $\hat{p}(u \mid ξ_t)$，這為行動空間賦予了指導規劃者梯度下降的黎曼幾何。<lb/>障礙曲率 $κ(ξ_t)$，即條件對數密度 $-\ln\hat{p}(\cdot\midξ_t)$ 的最小曲率，控制著收斂速率和安全邊際，取代了未知動力學的利普希茨常數。<lb/>我們的主要結果是一個上下文安全界限，顯示從真實可行性流形的距離受分數估計誤差和一個依賴於 $κ(ξ_t)$ 的比率控制，這兩者都隨著更豐富的上下文而改善。<lb/>在動態導航任務上的模擬確認，上下文PPC顯著超越邊際和固定密度模型，且在環境變化後優勢增強。
2604.19639.author: Tongxin Li et.al.
